trivia_bullets.text: Dismal Backwater originates in Khans of Tarkir (no. 232) and was reprinted in Fate Reforged with new art by Sam Burley depicting the same waterway location 1,280 years in the past. The Tarkir timeline conceit applies across all ten taplands in this cycle: Fate Reforged versions show the same places before the clans' full influence. [MTG Wiki: Fate Reforged; Wizards.com: 'A Fetching Look at Fate Reforged', 2014-12-24]
sources: Wizards.com: A Fetching Look at Fate Reforged (2014-12-24)

trivia_bullets.text: Dismal Backwater has an EDHREC rank of 949 and is legal in all currently active formats including Pauper and Standard. Scryfall records 20 printings as of 2026, including appearances in Duel Decks: Blessed vs. Cursed, Warhammer 40,000 Commander, and Tarkir: Dragonstorm. It is the most-reprinted of the four taplands in this batch, reflecting the ongoing demand for blue-black mana fixing. [Scryfall]

Sam Burley illustrated Dismal Backwater for both Khans of Tarkir and Fate Reforged, and returned as artist for Core Set 2019, Core Set 2020, Core Set 2021, Commander 2016, Commander 2017, Commander 2018, Eternal Masters, and Foundations. Sam Burley has also posted print-sale reproductions of both the KTK and FRF versions of this card on his INPRNT storefront. [Scryfall prints; INPRNT: samburley/dismal-backwater-frf]

related_cards.name: Dismal Backwater
related_cards.note: Original printing (no. 232); same oracle text, different art showing same location in present timeline

related_cards.name: Dimir Aqueduct
related_cards.note: Older blue-black dual land; enter-tapped bounce mechanic vs. life-gain; predecessor in the budget blue-black mana-fixing lineage
